programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: On June 11th, 1963, a man fishing in Oregon reels in the body of a toddler. The child had been wrapped in blankets and weighed down by iron molds.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Thirty years after college student Cathy Sposito is brutally killed on a hiking trail in Prescott, Arizona, can a groundbreaking forensic tool finally identify her killer?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1987, the body of Darlene Krashoc is discovered behind a Colorado Springs restaurant. She had been savagely beaten, raped and strangled. Investigators struggle to identify this barbaric killer. 30 years later, science provides a breakthrough.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: The NorCal Rapist terrorizes the women of North California for years until forensic science can identify him.

programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy is used to resolve the 1988 murder of a mother killed in front of her toddler.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy young woman was abducted in front of her in-laws' home, the police had no shortage of suspects. The victim left behind a tumultuous marriage, an ex-husband and several spurned lovers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the upscale town of Lodi, California, a 12-year-old girl was taken from her home. When the kidnapper's car was discovered in a field near the town, the FBI and local police teamed up with hundreds of volunteers in their search for the girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In February 1988, a record store clerk in Ohio was fatally shot in his van. After discovering thousands of dollars in the vehicle, police knew that robbery was not the motive.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In October 1993, a robber surprised unsuspecting customers by dropping through the roof of a California bank.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In August 1992, a man returned to his Memphis home and discovered that his wife was missing. The husband, his friends, family and neighbors began to receive ransom calls.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, the son of a successful real estate developer was kidnapped in Miami. After the father received a $3 million ransom demand from a self-proclaimed terrorist, the FBI became involved and agents began to suspect a pair of Colombian nationals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When notorious bank robber Byron Chubbuck escaped from New Mexico State Penitentiary in 2000, he almost immediately began stealing again. The FBI teamed up with U.S. Marshals to capture a resourceful criminal who proved he would never stop fighting.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June 1973, a young girl was abducted from a state park while camping with her family. A criminal profiler predicted that the kidnapper would contact the girl's family on the one-year anniversary of her abduction.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A drug dealer engaged in a shootout with Nebraska state troopers and evaded capture after a high-speed chase.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In January 1984, a U.S. Customs officer disappeared from his post near the Del Rio, Texas border crossing, prompting local police to search for four Latin American suspects. The following day, the official was found dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, Florida's cocaine imports were among the highest in the nation and regional territories were enforced with intimidation and murder. One dealer would stop at nothing to keep FBI agents from building a case against him.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Two robbers with assault rifles ambushed an armored car, killing both guards and seizing thousands of dollars.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a series of bank robberies in Texas baffled authorities. But when similar thefts occurred the next year in California, FBI agents linked the crimes and established a modus operandi.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In December 1981, a New Jersey state trooper was shot to death on the side of Interstate 80. Fingerprints on the suspect's vehicle pointed to a fugitive wanted on bank robbery charges.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stuart Campbell was convicted of the murder of his 15-year-old niece in 2002, even though her body has never been found.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jordan Monaghan killed his 24-day-old daughter Ruby on New Year's Day in January 2013. In August of that year, when his partner tried to end their relationship, he killed his son Logan, who was 21 months old.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Wayne Adam Ford is an American serial killer who strangled and dismembered three of his four victims. The first body was found in October 1997 and had been so thoroughly dismembered that her identity remains unknown.

programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a killer so evil that not even her own son could escape her clutches. She became the first woman to be executed in Florida since 1848.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2007, single mother of three Paige Birgfeld went missing. Police in Grand Junction, Colorado investigating her disappearance discovered she'd been secretly working as an escort and suspected one of her clients of abducting and killing her.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Michael Stephani has become etched in the minds of all who knew him as the notorious weepy voiced Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: Stephen Farrow committed two murders in early 2012 motivated by an obsessive hatred of Christianity, which he attributed to sexual abuse from a priest.

programme.desc: Jesse Con-ui's loyalty to the New Mexican Mafia drove him to betray and murder his former mentor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old girl disappeared from a small town in a remote area of Alaska, investigators wondered if she'd been attacked by a bear or become lost in the dense woods.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janet Overton died suspiciously in 1988, and the investigation hit a dead end when the autopsy indicated no foul play was involved. A telephone tip and the sensitive nose of a forensic examiner indicated otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a mini-vacation in Key West. The wife went missing, and police searched every square inch of the island, they found nothing but a pair of sandals which might have belonged to her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 62-year-old man opens a car restoration business and becomes seriously ill, his doctors cannot find the cause, but forensics can; identification of a highly toxic metal in the victim's blood leads to his poisoner.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An Alaskan police officer finds a woman's body while on routine patrol of a public park. During the autopsy, the medical examiner preserves the portion of her rib cage which sustained the fatal wound.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the mail-order bride of a former army sergeant goes missing, investigators look into the deaths of the man's previous two wives.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect became ill and died just before she was to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy revealed she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators had to determine which person who knew her had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Indiana police identified Richard Alexander as the "River Park Rapist" who assaulted four women that year. Later, DNA analysis casts doubt on his conviction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappears without a trace. Even though foul play is suspected, the leads don't pan out and the trail turns cold. Twenty years would pass before police are able to link a clump of hair discovered in an isolated area with the missing woman.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Chris Ochoa confessed to the 1988 rape and murder of Nancy DePriest, the case was closed. 8 years later, Achim Marino confessed to the same crime. Can DNA evidence identify the real perpetrator?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man who had committed crimes including murder was not caught. Almost 50 years later, advances in technology and handwriting analysis uncovered the criminal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom.

programme.desc: Authorities track a kidnapper who let his victim write a last will and informed the family of her murder.

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In this adaptation of a long-running British comedy series, Jamie, an unemployed photographer, can't figure out why he has so little luck with the ladies, and he doesn't get much insight from his roomie, Kevin, a manager at a security systems company. Kevin's girlfriend, Sarah, is a levelheaded nurse who sees the best in Kevin despite his chauvnistic ways, while Cherie is the hottie next door who is pursued by both Jamie and Kevin. Jamie later gets a new roommate, Steve, whose girlfriend, Katie, is an art teacher with a secret wild side.
